title: Arduino Programming Part I: Control Structures (Spiral Curriculum)
description: Learn Arduino control structures with progressive integration - each problem builds on previous concepts
scenario: You are programming an Arduino robot. Let's learn control structures step by step, then combine them increasingly.

teachingModules.title: Lesson 1: The if Statement

steps.explanation: if has three parts: the word 'if', a condition in parentheses, and actions in curly braces. It means: IF condition is true, THEN do the action.
Now we add a condition. 'temperature > 30' means 'temperature is greater than 30'. The '>' compares two values.
Inside the braces, we put the action: 'fanOn();'. This means: IF temperature > 30, THEN turn on the fan.

teachingModules.analogy: if is like a traffic light: IF green, THEN go. IF condition is true, THEN do action.
teachingModules.everydayExample: IF hungry, THEN eat. IF cold, THEN wear a jacket.

teachingModules.title: Lesson 2: Comparison Operators

content.explanation:
Comparison operators compare two values and return TRUE or FALSE.

> means GREATER THAN (5 > 3 is TRUE)
< means LESS THAN (3 < 5 is TRUE)
>= means GREATER OR EQUAL
<= means LESS OR EQUAL
== means EQUAL TO
!= means NOT EQUAL

teachingModules.analogy: Comparison operators are like a scale - they tell you which side is bigger.

teachingModules.title: Lesson 3: Two Choices with if...else

steps.explanation: First, the basic 'if'. If condition is TRUE, do action A.

steps.code:
if (light < 50) { LED = ON; }
else { LED = OFF; }
steps.explanation: else means 'OTHERWISE'. If condition is FALSE, do the else action instead. IF dim, THEN LED ON. ELSE, LED OFF.

teachingModules.analogy: if...else is like a fork: IF left, ELSE right. One path or the other.

teachingModules.title: Lesson 4: The for Loop

steps.explanation: for loop has three parts inside parentheses: start, condition, update. It repeats the action a set number of times.
Start at i=0. Keep going while i < 5. Add 1 to i each time. So it repeats 5 times (i=0,1,2,3,4).
This flashes the LED exactly 5 times. You know the exact count before starting.

teachingModules.analogy: for loop is like counting: FOR 5 times, do a push-up. You know exactly how many.

teachingModules.title: Lesson 5: The while Loop

steps.explanation: while loop: WHILE condition is true, keep doing the action. You don't know how many times - it depends on when the condition becomes false.
Keep moving forward WHILE no obstacle is detected. Stop when obstacle appears.

teachingModules.analogy: while is like waiting: WHILE raining, wait inside. Keep waiting until it stops.

understandingChecks.question: Can you explain 'if' in your own words?
understandingChecks.prompt: Try explaining it to a friend who has never programmed.

understandingChecks.question: for (i=0; i<3; i++) - how many times does it run?
When should you use while instead of for?
understandingChecks.answer: When you don't know how many times to repeat

spiralPractice.description: Cumulative practice: each problem combines previously learned concepts

problems.title: Practice: if Statement
problems.scenario: Temperature is 35°C. Write code to turn on fan if temp > 30.

feedback.incorrect: Remember: if (condition) - what condition means temp > 30?
Remember: for (start; condition; update) - how many times should it run?

practiceLevels.description: Combine 2 concepts: for loop + if statement

problems.title: Practice: for Loop + if Statement
problems.scenario: Flash LED 3 times, but ONLY if the temperature is above 25°C.
hints: First think: how do you check temperature?
Then think: how do you repeat something 3 times?
Put the for INSIDE the if, or the if INSIDE the for?

feedback.correct: Excellent! You're combining for and if together!
feedback.incorrect: Think: Do you want to flash 3 times IF temp is high? Or check temp EACH time you flash?

problems.title: Practice: while Loop + if Statement
problems.scenario: Keep moving forward UNTIL you detect an obstacle, then stop and check if it is close (< 20cm).

feedback.correct: Great! You combined while and if!
feedback.incorrect: Remember: while keeps going until condition is false. if checks a condition once.

practiceLevels.description: Combine 3 concepts: for loop + if + comparison

problems.title: Practice: for + if + Comparison
problems.scenario: Check 5 sensors. For EACH sensor that reads ABOVE 50, turn on its LED.
hints: Use for to loop through 5 sensors
Inside the loop, use if to check if sensor > 50
The comparison (>) checks if value is above threshold

feedback.incorrect: Break it down: 1) Loop 5 times, 2) Read each sensor, 3) If > 50, turn on LED

finalChallenge.title: FINAL MISSION: Arduino Robot Controller
finalChallenge.scenario: Design a complete Arduino program that demonstrates ALL the concepts you've learned.
requirements: Use a for loop to check all sensors on startup
Use if...else to respond to sensor readings differently
Use while loop for continuous monitoring
Use comparison operators to make decisions
Combine all concepts in one coherent program
finalChallenge.problem:
Create an Arduino program for an autonomous robot that:

1. On STARTUP (setup): Check all 4 sensors using a for loop
2. In the MAIN LOOP: 
   - Use while to keep the robot active
   - Use if...else to make decisions based on sensors:
     * If sensor reading > 80: Turn right
     * If sensor reading < 30: Turn left
     * Else: Go straight
   - Use comparison operators in your conditions
3. Use for loop inside to flash an LED 3 times when changing direction

Your program should demonstrate ALL concepts working together!
solutionApproach: 1. First, set up the for loop to initialize sensors
2. Then, create the while loop for continuous operation
3. Inside while, add if...else for decision making
4. Use comparison operators (>, <) in if conditions
5. Add a for loop inside the direction change to flash LED

evaluationCriteria.usesAllConcepts: Must use: for, while, if, if...else, comparison operators

feedback.perfect: PERFECT! You've mastered all concepts and can integrate them!
feedback.partial: Good progress! Review the concepts you missed and try again.
feedback.needsReview: Keep practicing! Go back to teaching modules for concepts you struggled with.

reward.message: Congratulations! You have completed the Spiral Curriculum for Arduino Control Structures!

metadata.sourceMaterial: Arduino Programming Part I - Control Structures (Lec14, ELEC1100 HKUST)
